Residential Window Cleaning in Armstrong: What You're Actually Dealing With

Armstrong's position in the Spallumcheen Valley places residential properties in a semi-arid agricultural basin where window contamination is driven by fine organic dust from dairy farming operations and dry-summer field work rather than lake proximity or forest pollen. The valley's dairy farms produce a particulate that is finer and lighter than standard field dust, allowing it to travel farther from source and settle on glass across a wider radius than coarser agricultural soils. New residential developments on the northeast and south edges of Armstrong — driven by buyers relocating from the Okanagan as property prices have risen — sit on freshly subdivided agricultural land where disturbed soil creates elevated dust during construction phases. Established homes on larger lots closer to the fairgrounds and downtown tend to have mature trees that provide some natural filtration, though these same trees contribute to organic debris buildup on sill edges and lower-story glass through late summer.

What is Residential Window Cleaning and Why Does It Matter in Armstrong?

Residential Window Cleaning in Armstrong involves removing the specific buildup that accumulates on glass in this area — pollen from surrounding forests, and the seasonal grime unique to the Shuswap's four-season climate. For Armstrong homeowners, clean windows aren't just cosmetic — they protect the glass surface from long-term etching and staining that becomes harder to remove over time.

A small city in the Spallumcheen Valley approximately 45 km south of Salmon Arm, at the junction of Hwys 97A and 97B. This geography shapes how windows age here. Armstrong's semi-arid valley position means hot, dry summers with significant agricultural dust. Late spring brings heavy Okanagan Valley pollen from the south.

Professional residential addresses these challenges systematically. Where DIY cleaning smears mineral deposits or misses the frame and sill buildup, a proper clean removes every layer — leaving glass that's genuinely clear rather than just less dirty.

For Armstrong's mix of suburban residential, agricultural properties, and small-town commercial — steady year-round demand for both residential and commercial cleaning., the investment in professional cleaning pays off both in appearance and in the long-term health of the glass itself.

What's Included in Our Residential Window Cleaning in Armstrong

Our residential in Armstrong is a complete clean from frame to sill — not just a wipe of the glass. Every job includes our standard process adapted to the specific conditions of your property, with a streak-free guarantee on every pane.

  • Exterior window cleaning — all accessible windows
  • Interior window cleaning — handled with care for your furnishings
  • Screen removal, cleaning, and reinstallation
  • Window track cleaning and wipe-down
  • Sill cleaning included at no extra charge
  • Streak-free squeegee finish on every pane

We work around your schedule and access requirements. For properties with multiple stories or restricted access points, we discuss the approach before starting and will tell you upfront if a ladder or extension pole is needed.

How We Do Residential Window Cleaning in Armstrong: Step by Step

Every Wagon Windows job follows the same process. Here's exactly what happens from the moment we arrive at your Armstrong property.

  1. 1. Walkthrough & Window Inventory

    Before touching anything, David walks the property with the homeowner to identify every window, flag any pre-existing damage, and confirm which areas are in-scope. This prevents disputes and ensures nothing is missed — especially on properties with skylights, hard-to-reach dormers, or custom glass panels common in Shuswap lakefront homes.

  2. 2. Screen Removal & Labelling

    Every screen is carefully removed and labelled by location — left-side bedroom, kitchen upper, etc. Labelling takes an extra minute per screen but eliminates the guesswork of reinstallation and prevents screens being forced into wrong frames, which damages splines. Screens are set aside in a safe, flat area during the clean.

  3. 3. Dry Debris Removal

    Before any water or solution is applied, loose debris — cottonwood fluff, dried insects, leaf fragments — is brushed away from frames, sills, and glass surfaces. Skipping this step turns debris into abrasive particles under the squeegee, which can scratch glass and leave streaks that require a second pass.

  4. 4. Pre-Rinse

    A light pre-rinse with clean water loosens mineral deposits, agricultural dust, and surface-level grime before the cleaning solution is applied. This is especially important on south-facing windows that accumulate UV-baked-on dust and pollen during Shuswap summers. Pre-rinsing reduces the work the solution has to do and extends its effectiveness.

  5. 5. Cleaning Solution Application

    A professional, eco-safe window cleaning solution is applied to the full pane using a wet applicator or sleeve mop. The solution lifts oils, mineral residue, and biological material that water alone cannot break down. Solution dwell time is calibrated to the level of buildup — heavier deposits get a longer soak before agitation.

  6. 6. Exterior Squeegee & Detail

    Each exterior pane is worked with a professional squeegee using overlapping, controlled strokes that pull solution and dissolved grime cleanly off the glass. Edges are hand-detailed with a lint-free cloth to catch any remaining moisture. Frames and sills are wiped down as part of this step — not as an afterthought.

  7. 7. Interior Window Cleaning

    Interior windows are cleaned with care for the home's furnishings — drop cloths or towels are placed on sills to catch drips, and solution is applied with a dampened applicator rather than a free-flowing mop to prevent overspray on walls or floors. Each pane is squeegeed and hand-detailed on the edges, with particular attention to upper corners where solution tends to pool.

  8. 8. Screen Reinstallation & Final Inspection

    Cleaned and dried screens are reinstalled by location using the labelling system from Step 2. David does a final walk around the property to inspect each window from the outside for any missed streaks or smears, correcting them on the spot. The homeowner is invited to do their own inspection before the job is signed off.

What sets us apart: Wagon Windows treats residential cleans as a complete service — screens labelled and reinstalled correctly, tracks wiped, and every window inspected before leaving — not a rushed rinse-and-go. David Hanna owns and runs the company himself, meaning the person on your property is accountable by name.

Common Residential Problems We Solve in Armstrong

  • Cottonwood pollen season coats every exterior window in a thick film that basic garden hose rinses cannot clear
  • Lake spray from Shuswap Lake deposits mineral-rich water on lakefront home windows that dries into white haze
  • Agricultural dust from nearby orchards and farms in the Salmon Arm corridor settles on glass and bakes on in summer heat
  • Wildfire smoke season leaves a greasy, oily film on glass that standard cleaning products struggle to break down
  • Interior condensation from high-humidity Shuswap summers causes mould and mineral buildup along window sills and lower frames

After a full residential clean, every window on the home is streak-free and clear — you notice the difference immediately when light comes through without the haze or smearing that built up over months. Screens sit flush in their frames, tracks are wiped clean, and the overall impression of the home from both inside and out is noticeably sharper. Most customers describe it as the house looking 'new again.'

When to Book Residential Window Cleaning in Armstrong

Most Armstrong properties benefit from residential 2–3 times per year: spring after pollen peaks (April–May), midsummer for dust and UV haze (July), and fall before freeze (September–October).

Armstrong's semi-arid valley position means hot, dry summers with significant agricultural dust. Late spring brings heavy Okanagan Valley pollen from the south.

Booking lead time for Armstrong: Because Armstrong is approximately 45 minutes from our Salmon Arm base, we schedule Armstrong jobs in batches when possible. We recommend booking at least 1–2 weeks in advance, especially during spring and fall peak periods.

To lock in your preferred date, get an instant estimate online or call us at (403) 837-5092. We'll confirm within a few hours.

Frequently Asked Questions — Residential in Armstrong

Does Armstrong's agricultural dust from the Spallumcheen Valley affect how often I should clean my windows?

Yes — Armstrong's dairy farming and crop agriculture in the Spallumcheen Valley means exterior glass accumulates grime faster than urban centres. Fine soil dust from field work and silage operations in summer is particularly aggressive on glass. Most Armstrong homeowners adjacent to farmland benefit from 3 cleans per year rather than two. The IPE fairgrounds in September also generates heavy dust in the surrounding neighbourhood — a post-fair clean in late September is worth considering for homes near the grounds.

I'm in a newer Armstrong subdivision — do new-build homes need different window cleaning?

New-build Armstrong homes often have construction residue — mortar splash, paint overspray, or sticker adhesive on glass — that standard cleaning won't address. If your home is less than 2 years old and hasn't been professionally cleaned since construction, flag this when booking. We carry a glass-safe solvent for adhesive and construction residue removal, quoted per affected window. Post-construction cleaning takes longer but sets up a clean baseline for ongoing maintenance.

How does the Okanagan Valley pollen that blows north into Armstrong affect exterior windows in spring?

Armstrong's position at the junction of Hwys 97A and 97B puts it directly in the path of southerly winds carrying Okanagan pollen north in late April and early May. The semi-arid pollen (from orchards and vineyards to the south) is finer and less sticky than Shuswap forest pollen but covers glass in a yellow-green film. A post-pollen clean in mid-May is ideal — waiting until June lets the pollen bake on during the first hot weeks, making it harder to remove.

What does exterior residential window cleaning typically cost for an Armstrong home?

Exterior-only cleaning for a standard Armstrong home runs $160–$300 depending on window count and story height. Armstrong has a mix of single-story bungalows (faster, lower cost) and two-story suburban builds (more equipment and time). Full interior and exterior service averages $250–$420 for mid-size homes. We're about 45 minutes from Salmon Arm, and our pricing reflects full-service value without a travel surcharge — the quote you get is the price you pay.

Do you clean both inside and outside windows for residential homes?

Yes — we offer exterior-only or full interior and exterior cleaning. Exterior-only is from $149 for small homes. Interior + exterior is from $199. Most customers choose full service for the best results.

Do I need to be home during the window cleaning?

For exterior-only cleaning, you don't need to be home as long as we have access to the outside of your property. For interior cleaning, someone should be present. Let us know your preference when booking.

Do you clean window screens and tracks for residential properties?

Screens and tracks are available as add-ons. Screens are $3–$4 each. Track cleaning starts at $50. These are often done at the same time as a window clean for the best results.

How often should I have my home's windows professionally cleaned?

Most Shuswap homeowners benefit from 2–3 cleans per year: spring (post-pollen), midsummer, and fall (pre-freeze). Lakefront homes may need an extra clean due to mineral spray.

Does Wagon Windows serve Armstrong?

Yes — Armstrong is about 45 minutes from our Salmon Arm base and within our regular service area. We serve residential homes and commercial businesses throughout Armstrong and the Spallumcheen Valley.

Should Armstrong homeowners book gutter cleaning before or after the IPE in September?

Book for October — after the Interior Provincial Exhibition (September) and after Armstrong's deciduous trees have dropped. Fall leaf-drop in Armstrong and Spallumcheen is heavy due to the valley's mature tree canopy. An October gutter clean captures the full fall deposit before freeze and prevents winter ice damming from blocked downspouts. Spring cleaning in June handles cottonwood and orchard pollen debris.

What areas does Wagon Windows serve?

Wagon Windows serves the entire Shuswap region from our Salmon Arm base: Salmon Arm, Sorrento, Tappen, Blind Bay, Sicamous, Chase, Enderby, and Armstrong. Contact us if your area isn't listed — we travel for the right job.

How do I get a quote from Wagon Windows?

The fastest way is to book a free 30-minute consultation via our Calendly link at wagonwindows.com — you'll have a confirmed appointment within minutes. You can also call or text (403) 837-5092, or email davidhanna@wagonwindow.com. We respond within a few hours.

Is Wagon Windows insured?

Yes — Wagon Windows carries liability insurance. We treat your property with care and take full responsibility for our work on every job.

What is the minimum job fee?

Our minimum job fee is $99. This covers smaller jobs, single-room requests, and add-ons like screen cleaning or track cleaning on their own.
